summary: DHH praises Danish telecom Telmore for its radically simple pricing model and flat organizational structure. With 300,000 customers gained through transparent pricing—zero subscription cost, flat SMS and per-minute rates—Telmore stands in contrast to legacy telcos with confusing service schemes. DHH highlights that when he emailed the company, he received a detailed reply within hours and was told his message would reach the CEO. He sees this minimal hierarchy as the key to Telmore delivering better, cheaper service.
key_points: Simple, transparent pricing beats complex 'adjustable' service schemes that make comparison shopping difficult
Flat organizational structures with minimal layers between customers and leadership produce better responsiveness
Telmore's direct customer-to-CEO communication pipeline is a competitive advantage over legacy telcos
Companies that maintain constant contact with customers can deliver more value at lower cost
money_quotes: I have great respect for a company that installs just a single guard between the customer and the CEO.
No wonder Telmore is able to provide more, better, and cheaper. They're in constant report with their customers.
That's an attractive proposition in a market littered with 'adjustable' service schemes that heavily tax any comparison between the telcos.
key_insight: Companies that strip away organizational layers and pricing complexity can outperform entrenched competitors by staying directly connected to their customers.
